CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Set For Trial On May-08-28 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO FLEET WEEK ASSOCIATION, and UMBRELLA ENTERTAINMENT GROUP'S, DBA THE AIR SHOW, MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Specially appearing defendants City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Fleet Week Association and Umbrella Entertainment Group motion to admit Amber Hemberger pro hac vice is granted. Good cause shown and the conditions of the conditional opposition filed by plaintiffs lack merit. The additional information sought by plaintiffs is not required by CRC 9.40 or, as far as the court is aware, any California authority.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO FLEET WEEK ASSOCIATION, and UMBRELLA ENTERTAINMENT GROUP'S, DBA THE AIR SHOW, MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Specially appearing defendants City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Fleet Week Association and Umbrella Entertainment Group motion to admit Eric Cunningham pro hac vice is granted. Good cause shown and the conditions of the conditional opposition filed by plaintiffs lack merit. The additional information sought by plaintiffs is not required by CRC 9.40 or, as far as the court is aware, any California authority.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
